stories.title: DeepSeek v4
stories.author: impact_sy
stories.one_line_summary: DeepSeek V4-Pro 1.6T MoE / 49B active and V4-Flash 284B / 13B active released with weights on Hugging Face, 1M context, trained on Huawei Ascend hardware end-to-end.
stories.comment_highlight: antirez: Actually the fact the inference of a SOTA model is completely Nvidia-free is the biggest attack to Nvidia ever carried so far. Even American frontier AI labs may start to buy Chinese hardware if they need to continue the AI race.

stories.title: Google plans to invest up to $40B in Anthropic
stories.author: elffjs
stories.one_line_summary: Bloomberg reports $10B cash now at $350B valuation plus $30B performance-gated, mostly delivered as Google Cloud credits, deepening the cloud-credits-as-equity pattern after Amazon's similar Anthropic deal.
stories.comment_highlight: htrp: Google is committing $10 billion now in cash at a $350 billion valuation and will invest a further $30 billion if Anthropic meets performance targets. How much of this goes back to Google as cloud spend?

stories.title: An update on recent Claude Code quality reports
stories.author: mfiguiere
stories.one_line_summary: Anthropic publishes engineering post acknowledging three concrete Claude Code regressions: default thinking changed March from high to medium without disclosure, resumed sessions had thinking tokens stripped, and system prompt drift; remediation took 2-6 weeks per item.
stories.comment_highlight: jryio: 1. They changed the default in March from high to medium, however Claude Code still showed high (took 1 month 3 days to notice and remediate). 2. Old sessions had the thinking tokens stripped, resuming the session made Claude stupid (took 15 days to notice and remediate).

stories.title: OpenAI releases GPT-5.5 and GPT-5.5 Pro in the API
stories.author: arabicalories
stories.one_line_summary: OpenAI ships GPT-5.5 and Pro to the public API one day after the consumer launch, with native MCP via Responses API, computer use, hosted shell, apply patch, and Skills.
stories.comment_highlight: throw03172019: Faster than anticipated because of Deepseek release?

stories.title: I cancelled Claude: Token issues, declining quality, and poor support
stories.author: y42
stories.one_line_summary: Builder rage-quit post detailing 32K output token cap hit at 53 minutes of Sonnet medium-effort thinking, full session limit consumed, lands on front page same day as Anthropic's quality mea-culpa.
stories.comment_highlight: minimaxir: These changes fixed some of the token issues, but the token bloat is an intrinsic problem to the model, and Anthropic's solution of defaulting to xhigh reasoning for Opus 4.7 just means you'll go through tokens faster anyways.

stories.title: Show HN: A Karpathy-style LLM wiki your agents maintain (Markdown and Git)
stories.author: najmuzzaman
stories.one_line_summary: Local wiki layer for agents with markdown plus git as source of truth, BM25 search via bleve plus SQLite index, runs at ~/.wuphf/wiki/, no vector or graph DB.
stories.comment_highlight: najmuzzaman: The shape is the one Karpathy has been circling for a while - an LLM-native knowledge substrate that agents both read from and write into, so context compounds across sessions.

stories.title: Open source memory layer so any AI agent can do what Claude.ai and ChatGPT do
stories.author: alash3al
stories.one_line_summary: Stash ships an Apache-2.0 memory layer with MCP server, 28 tools, and background consolidation so any agent gets the same long-term memory as Claude.ai or ChatGPT plus.
stories.comment_highlight: alash3al: Platform memory is locked to one model and one company. Stash brings the same capability to any agent - local, cloud, or custom. MCP server, 28 tools, background consolidation, Apache 2.0.

stories.title: Show HN: Browser Harness - Gives LLM freedom to complete any browser task
stories.author: gregpr07
stories.one_line_summary: Browser-Use team strips heuristics layer (~tens of thousands of LOC over Chrome CDP) and exposes raw browser to the LLM with self-correction and tool synthesis, MIT-licensed.
stories.comment_highlight: gregpr07: We got tired of browser frameworks restricting the LLM, so we removed the framework and gave the LLM maximum freedom. Browser Use is tens of thousands of lines of deterministic heuristics wrapping Chrome.

frontier_alerts: Mythos breach: zero new HN activity in 72h window. No regulatory or community response surfaced.
OpenAI Workspace Agents: 60h old, outside 24h window. Initial HN reception lukewarm (51 + 160 pts).
Google Cloud Next 2026 aftermath: no fresh activity in 24h - story already digested by community on launch days.
Meta-pattern: three of top 5 stories are about Anthropic/Claude (40B deal + quality update + cancellation post) - signals broader narrative shift around Claude reliability.

anchor_followup_status.deepseek_v4: MAJOR - 1962 pts, 1503 comments, antirez + simonw substantive engagement, weights live on HF
anchor_followup_status.gpt55_api: STRONG - 247 pts on API release post (consumer launch post 1552 pts on April 23)
anchor_followup_status.mythos_breach: NO ACTIVITY - zero qualifying stories in window
anchor_followup_status.google_anthropic_40b: STRONG - 644 pts top story, dependency-on-cloud-credits framing dominates
